Patch Set 2: Verified+1

Passed enforced test review-ldiskfs-dne-arm on RHEL 8.7/aarch64, RHEL 8.7/x86_64 uploaded by Onyx Autotest from onyx-91vm5: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/ae48e96a-1e1e-4c4f-b2b4-fb4bd566c574 ran 5 tests.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

There probably isn't a ton of value to run static analysis on the test programs. They are not installed or run on user systems normally, and are short lived so they have had very little effort beyond just making them run. 

If this makes your analysis tool happy, and allows it to be run tree-wide more easily, then I'm happy to land it, but I suspect your time would be better used elsewhere in the kernel code or user tools than in the test code.
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1
> 
> There probably isn't a ton of value to run static analysis on the test programs. They are not installed or run on user systems normally, and are short lived so they have had very little effort beyond just making them run. 
> 
> If this makes your analysis tool happy, and allows it to be run tree-wide more easily, then I'm happy to land it, but I suspect your time would be better used elsewhere in the kernel code or user tools than in the test code.

The analyzer is a Clang plugin, so it warned on everything that gets compiled on my setup. The only major omissions are snmp (since it doesn't get built by default and isn't actively developed), ldiskfs (since it's tough to built against newer mainline kernels), and several of the LNDs.

The plugin runs over the whole tree without any false-positives. I have patches for the rest of the warnings. I'll also find a way to force compile the rest of the tree to get those too.

Patch Set 2:

I am not sure this is a good application of effort to make test cases all nice and super tidy like this. It's not the code that everybody would be running in production after all, or link with (why would you care about the namespace pollution otherwise)

Of course it does not hurt to land this, but I just think in the future there are better areas where time could be spent.
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 629d6bca95f96bd307d6a9da9b04d73d3fe7c68f by Oleg Drokin

Patch Set 3:

> I am not sure this is a good application of effort to make test
 > cases all nice and super tidy like this. It's not the code that
 > everybody would be running in production after all, or link with
 > (why would you care about the namespace pollution otherwise)
 > 
 > Of course it does not hurt to land this, but I just think in the
 > future there are better areas where time could be spent.

Oleg, I said the same in my earlier review comment. I think the main benefit to land such a patch is to quiet spurious warnings from static code analysis tools so that the real bugs can be found more easily.

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> Oleg, I said the same in my earlier review comment. I think the main benefit to land such a patch is to quiet spurious warnings from static code analysis tools so that the real bugs can be found more easily.

Well, I guess bugs in test tools do exist too

commit.subject: LU-8191 tests: convert functions to static
commit.message:
LU-8191 tests: convert functions to static

Static analysis shows that a number of functions
could be made static. This patch declares several
functions in various test helpers static.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>
Change-Id: I065fb4398ed1670ce6ad58cf946054f6bd1ec282
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/51433
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Petros Koutoupis <petros.koutoupis@hpe.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
